title: SMSociety 2026 Conference Programme
snippet: An event announcement and program guide for the International Conference on Social Media & Society in Glasgow. Fabio Giglietto is listed as a featured panelist for the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable – Where to for Social Media Research?'.
description: Fabio Giglietto is featured as a roundtable panelist discussing the Meta Content Library and the future of social media research at the International Conference on Social Media & Society.

title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time
snippet: An in-depth media blog post covering the 2026 International Communication Association (ICA) conference in Cape Town. It details a presentation by Fabio Giglietto on how partisan alignment, journalistic quality, and Meta's algorithmic interventions shape what news millions of users see on Facebook.
description: A detailed blog post covering Fabio Giglietto's presentation at the ICA 2026 conference, analyzing how Meta's algorithmic tweaks impact user engagement with news URLs on Facebook.

title: L'articolo 21 nella gabbia di Facebook
snippet: An essay on Terzogiornale exploring constitutional free speech in the modern digital age. The author cites Fabio Giglietto's extensive analysis of over 2.5 million Facebook posts to criticize how massive private tech companies regulate public and political discourse, often with counterproductive effects on democratic communication.
description: A journalistic analysis discussing Fabio Giglietto's computational social science research on Facebook algorithmic visibility drops for Italian parliamentarians and its implications for digital freedom of speech.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: An article detailing the findings of Fabio Giglietto’s research. It outlines how algorithmic suppression designed to limit 'political content' on Meta's feeds was ineffective against extremist, pro-Russian, and anti-EU accounts, causing them to become the leading informational ecosystem on Facebook in Italy.
description: An article covering Professor Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts, focusing on how political extremist pages bypassed Meta's filters to gain visibility during elections.

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: This news article covers a research study led by Professor Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino. The study analyzed over 2.5 million Facebook posts to demonstrate how Meta's algorithms have suppressed official political voices while inadvertently boosting extremist and anti-European accounts, prompting Italian political parties to propose bipartisan regulation [1.4.3].
description: An article detailing a research study co-authored by Fabio Giglietto on how Meta's algorithmic changes affect the organic reach of political groups in Italy.

title: ICA 2026 Annual Conference: Communication and Inequalities in Context
snippet: The official schedule and speaker listing for the International Communication Association (ICA) Annual Conference in June 2026, which lists Fabio Giglietto as a presenting scholar. Giglietto is scheduled to speak on topics evaluating political efficacy and longitudinal information selection under algorithmic feeds.
description: Fabio Giglietto is listed as a presenting scholar at the ICA 2026 Annual Conference, where he is scheduled to speak on political efficacy and algorithmic feeds.

title: Referendum. Bonelli e Fratoianni (Avs) scrivono al Presidente Agcom, preoccupati per le fake news veicolate sui social
snippet: Italian political leaders Angelo Bonelli and Nicola Fratoianni cite Professor Fabio Giglietto’s study, 'Meta's Political Content Reduction Policy and Italian Parliamentarians' Facebook Visibility', in an official letter to the Agcom regulatory authority to voice concerns over algorithmic biases that penalize institutional politicians.
description: Italian political leaders cite a study by Professor Fabio Giglietto regarding Meta's political content reduction policy in an official letter to the Agcom regulatory authority.

title: Referendum. Bonelli e Fratoianni (Avs) scrivono al Presidente Agcom, preoccupati per le fake news veicolate sui social...
snippet: In a formal press release and letter addressed to the President of AGCOM (Italy’s communications regulatory authority), political leaders from the Alleanza Verdi e Sinistra party cite Professor Fabio Giglietto's study on Meta's political content reduction policy as expert evidence of algorithmic bias and the systemic penalization of mainstream democratic representatives online.
description: An official press release from the Italian political party Alleanza Verdi e Sinistra citing Professor Fabio Giglietto's study on Meta's algorithmic policies in a formal letter to AGCOM.
